name: Ben Sasse
summary: Former Republican Senator who denounced efforts to overturn the 2020 election as a dangerous ploy, called colleagues 'institutional arsonists,' and voted to convict Trump in his second impeachment trial.

keyStatements.quote: The president and his allies are playing with fire... But the president doesn’t and neither do the institutional arsonist members of Congress who will object to the Electoral College vote.
keyStatements.context: Facebook post on objections to Electoral College certification

keyStatements.quote: Adults don't point a loaded gun at the heart of legitimate self-government.
I agree with that. President Trump violated his oath of office to protect and defend the Constitution of the United States.
keyStatements.context: Senate floor remarks during impeachment trial

timeline.significance: Wrote in Mike Pence for president

timeline.event: Condemned effort to overturn the 2020 election
Voted to convict Trump in second impeachment
timeline.significance: One of seven Republicans to vote for conviction
Became University of Florida president in 2023

timeline.event: Resigned as University of Florida president

credibility.background: Former U.S. Senator, university president, historian with PhD from Yale

oppositionReasons: Said efforts to overturn the 2020 election were 'playing with fire'
Called objecting members of Congress 'institutional arsonists'
Said Trump violated his oath of office
endorsement: Voted to convict Trump; did not support Trump in 2016 or 2020

sources.title: Sasse slams 'institutional arsonist members of Congress' who plan Electoral College objection
GOP senators say they will reject election results unless commission is formed
Board of Trustees selects Dr. Ben Sasse as the University of Florida’s 13th president
University of Florida President Ben Sasse announces resignation
